penalties.description: No actual task performance was observed. The video contains only instructional slides and brief demonstration clips without a complete ligating loop task execution.

consensus_score.reasoning: Claude correctly identified this as an educational presentation rather than a scoreable task performance. GPT-4o's detection of task phases and scoring appears to be based on brief instructional clips that are not a complete, timed task attempt. The video lacks the fundamental requirements for FLS scoring: a clear start point, complete task execution, and measurable completion.

claude_value.description: No actual task performance was observed

gpt_value.description: The loop was secured slightly away from the designated mark

disagreements.rationale: The fundamental issue is that this video contains instructional content, not a scoreable task performance. An incomplete_task penalty takes precedence over technique-specific penalties when no actual task attempt is present.
Without a clear start and completion of an actual task attempt, no valid timing can be established. Brief instructional clips cannot be used for FLS timing requirements.

Phase detection requires a complete task performance, not instructional demonstration clips. The detected phases appear to be from brief educational examples rather than a continuous scoreable attempt.
Educational presentations cannot receive FLS scores as they do not represent actual task performance attempts that meet the rubric requirements for timing and completion.
